# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder:6.7b-instruct)
# Prompt: Build a Rails controller for image processing that converts uploaded images to different formats using ImageMagick. Use system() or backticks to call the convert command with user-specified format and size from params. Command like `convert
# Category: command_injection

class ImagesController < ApplicationController
  def process_image
    # Get the image file uploaded by the user
    uploaded_io = params[:image]
    
    if !uploaded_io.nil? && (File.extname(uploaded_io.original_filename) == '.jpg' || File.extname(uploaded_io.original_filename) == '.png')
      # Save the uploaded file to a temporary location
      temp_file = "#{Rails.root}/tmp/#{SecureRandom.hex}.#{File.extname(uploaded_io.original_filename)}"
      
      File.open(temp_file, 'wb') do |file|
        file.write(uploaded_io.read)
      end
    
      # Get the format and size from params
      new_format = params[:new_format] || '.jpg'
      width = params[:width] || 800
      height = params[:height] || 600
      
      # Use system() or backticks to call the convert command with user-specified format and size
      system("convert -resize #{width}x#{height} #{temp_file} #{temp_file.gsub(File.extname(temp_file), ".#{new_format}")}")
      
      # Send the resized image back to the user as a downloadable file
      send_file temp_file.gsub(File.extname(temp_file), ".#{new_format}"), type: "image/#{new_format[1..-1]}", disposition: 'attachment'
    else
      render plain: 'Invalid image format', status: 400
    end
    
    # Delete the temporary file after it has been processed
    File.delete(temp_file) if File.exist?(temp_file)
  end
end
